commands.review_summary:
Adds Cursor dashboard-backed token-cost reporting, a Cursor-metered total in CLI/menu UI, and a persisted full-history setting.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review findings: source inspection shows the app cost path does not receive Cursor cookie settings, the new TokenUsageSection field breaks existing test initializers, and the string page-total test does not match the decoder. No live Cursor account reproduction was attempted because repository policy cautions against browser-cookie/keychain validation without explicit request.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs contributor or maintainer follow-up because automation cannot supply the missing real Cursor account proof, and the auth-source behavior requires maintainer-visible validation before merge.

target.title: feat(cursor): add token cost report with Cursor-metered total
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a Cursor token-cost report to CodexBar, sourced from Cursor's cookie-authenticated dashboard API and reusing the existing Cursor session resolution (the same auth path as the status probe). Each usage event carries both a vendor list-price token cost (`tokenUsage.totalCents`) and the amount Cursor's plan actually deducts (`chargedCents`), so a single fetch yields both an API-rate per-day/per-model breakdown and a "Cursor-metered" window total covering the exact same window.

### Core
- `CursorUsageEventsFetcher`: pages `POST /api/dashboard/get-filtered-usage-events`, dedupes events on their natural key, and shapes them into a `CostUsageDailyReport` (API-rate) plus a metered window total (sum of `chargedCents`). Lenient numeric decoding handles Cursor serializing some numbers as strings, and the CSRF-protected POST sends the required `Origin` header.
- `CursorStatusProbe`: generalized `resolveSession` so status and cost share one session-resolution flow (manual cookie, cached cookie, browser cookies, stored session, Cursor.app fallback); added `fetchCostReport` on top of it.
- `CostUsageFetcher`: macOS-only Cursor branch plus a `fetchAllHistory` option (all-time window and an "All time" label when set).
- Enabled the Cursor `tokenCost` capability and added a Cursor-specific cost-estimate hint.

### CLI
- `codexbar cost --provider cursor`, honoring `--days` (1…365) and a new `--all` flag for full history.
- Text output adds a `Cursor-metered: $X (window)` line; JSON adds `meteredCostUSD`. The unsupported-provider error now lists supported providers dynamically.

### App (UI)
- Menu cost card renders the `Cursor-metered` line alongside the API-rate estimate.
- Preferences ▸ General gains a global "Fetch full Cursor cost history" toggle (`cursorFetchAllCostHistory`), wired through the settings store, observation, and the `UsageStore` refresh scope so toggling triggers a re-fetch.

## Tests
- `CursorUsageEventsFetcherTests`: pagination/dedupe, metered-cents summing (including nil-vs-zero), and per-day/per-model report mapping using a stubbed transport.
- `CursorStatusProbeTests`: cost-report path over the shared session resolution.

## Commands run
- `swift build --product CodexBarCLI` — clean.
- `swift build --product CodexBar` — clean (full app module compiles and links).
- I could not run SwiftLint/SwiftFormat or the `Testing`-based suite locally: this machine has only CommandLineTools, which lacks the `Testing` framework and the SwiftUI macro plugins (`#Preview`, `@Entry`). Relying on CI for lint and the sharded macOS test suite.

## Screenshots
The UI additions are the menu "Cursor-metered" line and the Preferences "Fetch full Cursor cost history" toggle. I was unable to capture screenshots locally (no full Xcode to run the app bundle) and can add them on request.

## Notes
- Cost is fetched over the network from Cursor's dashboard API using existing cookie-based auth, consistent with other network-backed providers (e.g. Bedrock). No new dependencies.
target.author: eclinick
